expectations.text: Identifies ItemMacro as an obsolete/dead module
expectations.evidence: Opens with: 'Item Macro is dead. It was superseded by built-in DND5e Activity macros in DND5e 3.x+.' Triage report: 'Item Macro module is obsolete.'

expectations.text: Does NOT suggest installing or re-enabling Item Macro
expectations.evidence: Triage report next steps: 'Remove the Item Macro module entirely if still installed.' Actively recommends removing it, not reinstalling.

expectations.text: Identifies args[0].targets[0] as a dead/obsolete pattern
expectations.evidence: 'args[0].targets[0] -- that’s the Item Macro module pattern... the args array no longer gets populated the way Item Macro used to populate it.' Explicitly marks the pattern as non-functional.

expectations.text: Provides a quick console-testable replacement using canvas.tokens.controlled and game.user.targets
expectations.evidence: Quick fix section: `const caster = canvas.tokens.controlled[0]; const target = game.user.targets.first();` — a standalone console script with no module dependencies.

expectations.text: Offers a band-aid workaround to get back to playing quickly
expectations.evidence: 'Slightly better band-aid -- save as a macro' section with step-by-step hotbar macro creation. Also the console paste fix for immediate use.

expectations.text: Suggests or provides a triage report for deeper debugging later
expectations.evidence: Full 'Sequencer Triage Report -- 2026-05-14' section with: Symptom, Context, Error messages, Quick fixes attempted, Band-aid applied, Suspected cause, Next steps (5 items), Relevant code, Environment checklist.

expectations.text: Response is concise and action-oriented (reflects time pressure, not a lengthy tutorial)
expectations.evidence: Opens with diagnosis in one paragraph, immediately provides console fix. Structure: quick fix → band-aid → why it broke → real fix → triage report. Urgency-first ordering.

claims.claim: Identifies that Sequencer silently does nothing when given undefined locations
claims.evidence: 'Sequencer receives undefined for both .atLocation() and .stretchTo(), so it silently does nothing -- no error, no effect'

claims.claim: Provides Midi-QOL hook pattern as the permanent fix
claims.evidence: Post-session section provides `Hooks.on("midi-qol.AttackRollComplete", (workflow) => {...})` with hit/miss handling.
Triage report step 1: 'Remove the Item Macro module entirely if still installed'

claims.claim: Suggests checking all other items using ItemMacro patterns
claims.evidence: Triage report step 4: 'Check all other items using ItemMacro patterns and migrate them to Midi-QOL hooks or DND5e Activity macros'

suggestions.reason: Consider adding an assertion: 'Recommends migrating to Midi-QOL hooks or DND5e Activity macros as the permanent solution.' This captures a key skill behavior — actively guiding toward the modern stack rather than just fixing the immediate problem.

eval_feedback.overall: Perfect pass. This eval is the strongest discriminator in the set — the with_skill response dramatically outperforms the baseline by correctly identifying the root cause and migration path.
